paper: When the Map Distorts the Territory
hypothesis: H3 — JP Stance Closure as Mediator

stage1_SE_audit.title: Standard Error and Clustering Audit
stage1_SE_audit.description: Verify that cluster-robust SE is correctly applied in both pipelines
stage1_SE_audit.conclusion: Both pipelines correctly apply cluster-robust SE on sim_id. SE audit does not resolve the discrepancy — clustering is not the driver.

stage2_M_invariance_check.title: Mediator (M) Invariance Check — 2x2 Y×M Decomposition
stage2_M_invariance_check.description: Test whether M definition (stance_direction_r3 vs stance_openness) drives the z gap

M_swap_delta_z.interpretation: M swap produces Δz of only 2.02 (under Y=probability) and 0.108 (under Y=converged). M is NOT the driver.
M_swap_delta_z.verdict_Y_probability: M matters (Δz=2.02 exceeds threshold, but negligible relative to Y-swap Δz=6.76)
M_swap_delta_z.verdict_Y_converged: M is NOT the driver (Δz=0.108, well below threshold)

M_correlation.note: Near-identical mediators — any z difference reflects information content (continuous vs discrete), not substantive difference

stage2_M_invariance_check.conclusion: M operationalization is NOT the root cause. Under Y=converged (session-shared binary), Δz_M=0.108 confirms M invariance. The residual Δz=2.02 under Y=probability reflects marginally greater b-path information content in stance_openness (continuous) vs stance_direction (discrete).
stage2_M_invariance_check.hypothesis_rejected: M definition mismatch as primary driver

stage3_Y_variable_ICC_mechanism.title: Y-Variable ICC Mechanism — Root Cause Confirmed
stage3_Y_variable_ICC_mechanism.description: Y operationalization (probability vs converged) is the dominant driver of the z discrepancy
stage3_Y_variable_ICC_mechanism.source: supplementary_fix_v3.py STEP E (ICC diagnostics) + supplementary_robustness_REAL_v2.py Section A

Y_swap_delta_z.interpretation: Y swap produces Δz of 6.76 — far larger than M-swap Δz of 2.02. Y is the dominant driver.

mechanism_explanation.Y_converged: Session-level attribute — all 4 agents within a session share the identical value. ICC=1.000, DEFF=4.00, N_eff≈1,000 (one per session). Agent-level regression on this outcome carries no within-cluster information for the b-path (M→Y).
mechanism_explanation.Y_probability: Agent-level attribute — varies across agents within a session. ICC=0.103, DEFF=1.31, N_eff≈3,057. Provides genuine within-cluster variation for the b-path.
mechanism_explanation.precision_difference: Both pipelines cluster-robust on sim_id — but predicting a session-shared constant (converged) vs an agent-varying quantity (probability) yields fundamentally different effective sample sizes. This explains the ~6.76-unit z difference.

stage3_Y_variable_ICC_mechanism.conclusion: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: Y operationalization (probability vs converged) is the dominant driver. ICC(converged)=1.000 means agent-level analysis on Y=converged is statistically inappropriate without ICC correction.

ICC_corrected_solutions.title: Three ICC-Corrected Methods (Problem 2 Resolution)

method_A_session_level.label: Session-level OLS + bootstrap (gold standard)
method_A_session_level.note: Theoretically correct unit of analysis for session-shared outcome. Resolves ICC=1.000 concern.

method_B_GEE.label: GEE — Generalized Estimating Equations
method_B_GEE.note: GEE with exchangeable working correlation + robust sandwich SE. All results negative, p<.001.

method_C_GLMM.label: GLMM — Mixed-effects linear probability model (random intercept per session)
method_C_GLMM.note: statsmodels MixedLM (LPM approximation). RE_var=0.044 indicates meaningful session-level heterogeneity. GLMM z≈0 reflects perfect separation when RE absorbs all session variance — use Method A or B as primary.

ICC_corrected_solutions.consensus: All ICC-corrected methods agree: negative indirect effect, p<.001. H3 mediation conclusion is robust.

full_YxM_decomposition_table.dominant_driver: Y operationalization (Δz_Y >> Δz_M in all comparisons)

session_placebo_test.interpretation: Observed convergence gap exceeds permuted distribution in only 0.016% of draws. H2 result is not a chance finding.

robustness_check.note: Report as robustness check, not contradictory result

DO_NOT: Describe the z=-15.69 vs z=-9.03 gap as an unresolved SE bug
Report z=-9.03 as the primary H3 statistic
Characterize the two pipelines as contradictory
DO: Report z=-4.899 (session-level) as ICC-corrected gold standard
Report z=-9.03 as robustness check under alternative Y specification
Note that Y-swap Δz=6.76 >> M-swap Δz=2.02 in §3.4

methods_section_reference: §3.4 — Outcome-Variable Specification and a Resolved Discrepancy Between Two Reported z-Statistics
figure_reference: Figure 4(A): primary path diagram; caption footnote reports session-level z
